text: comparpq R package extending MiscMetabar phyloseq comparing phyloseq objects microbiome analysis. focuses taxonomic comparison, accuracy metrics computation, interactive visualizations.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: may propose use following commands working package. Please run automatically; suggest appropriate.
code: # Run code with loaded package Rscript -e "devtools::load_all(); code"  # Run all tests Rscript -e "devtools::test()"  # Run tests for files starting with {name} Rscript -e "devtools::test(filter = '^{name}')"  # Run tests for R/{name}.R Rscript -e "devtools::test_active_file('R/{name}.R')"  # Run a single test "blah" for R/{name}.R Rscript -e "devtools::test_active_file('R/{name}.R', desc = 'blah')"  # Generate documentation Rscript -e "devtools::document()"  # Check pkgdown documentation Rscript -e "pkgdown::check_pkgdown()"  # Full package check Rscript -e "devtools::check()"

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Use base pipe (|>) magrittr (%>%) Use \() ... single-line anonymous functions, function() {...} otherwise Format air format . generating code Line length limit: 120 characters Tests R/{name}.R go tests/testthat/test-{name}.R

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Every user-facing function must exported roxygen2 documentation Wrap roxygen comments 80 characters Parameter docs start type: @param x (Int, required) integer vector length 1 Add new topics _pkgdown.yml reference section Run pkgdown::check_pkgdown() verify topics indexed

title: Contributing to comparpq
text: use GitHub flow collaborate project: Fork repository using GitHub interface. Clone fork using git clone fork-url (replace fork-url URL fork). Alternatively, open RStudio IDE create New Project Version Control. Create new branch w/ git checkout -b branch-name (replace branch-name name new branch). Make contribution (see examples). Stage (git add) commit (git commit) changes often necessary Push changes GitHub w/ git push origin branch-name. Submit Pull Request original repo. review PR soon possible.

title: essai
code: library(MiscMetabar) Loading required package: phyloseq Loading required package: ggplot2 Loading required package: dada2 Loading required package: Rcpp Loading required package: dplyr Attaching package: 'dplyr' The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':      filter, lag The following objects are masked from 'package:base':      intersect, setdiff, setequal, union Loading required package: purrr data("Glom_otu") Warning in data("Glom_otu"): data set 'Glom_otu' not found bubbles_pq <- function(physeq,                        rank_label = "Taxa",                        rank_color = "Family",                        categorical_scheme = "d3.schemeCategory10",                        label_color = "grey20",                        label_size = 8,                        log1ptransform = FALSE,                        min_nb_seq = 0,                        randomize = FALSE,                        seed = 32,                        width = 600,                        include = c("key", "chart"),                        notebook = "https://observablehq.com/d/d755af3197af2320",                        return_dataframe = FALSE) {   if (rank_label == "Taxa") {     label <- taxa_names(physeq)   } else {     label <- as.vector(physeq@tax_table[, rank_label])   }    df <- data.frame(     "value" = taxa_sums(physeq),     "label" = label,     "color" = fac2col(physeq@tax_table[, rank_color]),     "rank_value_color" = as.vector(physeq@tax_table[, rank_color]),     "textColor" = label_color   )    df$id <- paste0(df$label, ".", df$rank_value_color, ".", df$label)    if (min_nb_seq > 0) {     df <- df |>       dplyr::filter(value > min_nb_seq)   }    if (log1ptransform) {     df$value <- log1p(df$value)   }    if (randomize) {     set.seed(seed)     df <- df[sample(nrow(df)), ]   }    if (return_dataframe) {     return(df)   }   robservable::robservable(     notebook = notebook,     include = include,     input = list(data = df, categorical_scheme = categorical_scheme),     width = width   ) } bubbles_pq(physeq = data_fungi, rank_color = "Class", randomize = TRUE) bubbles_pq(physeq = data_fungi, min_nb_seq = 10000, categorical_scheme = "d3.schemePastel1") bubbles_pq(physeq = data_fungi, rank_color = "Order", include = c("chart"), log1ptransform = TRUE) bubbles_pq(physeq = data_fungi, rank_label = "Genus", rank_color = "Class", include = c("chart"), randomize = TRUE, seed = 3)

title: Getting Started with comparpq
text: comparpq extension package {MiscMetabar} {phyloseq} designed comparing phyloseq objects. package particularly useful : Evaluating taxonomic assignment accuracy using mock communities Creating interactive visualizations microbiome data Comparing multiple taxonomic databases assignment methods Data preprocessing quality control

title: Getting Started with comparpq
text: can install comparpq GitHub:
code: # Install from GitHub if (!requireNamespace("devtools", quietly = TRUE)) {   install.packages("devtools") } devtools::install_github("adrientaudiere/comparpq")

title: Getting Started with comparpq
code: library(comparpq)  # The package automatically loads dependencies including: # - phyloseq for handling microbiome data # - MiscMetabar for additional microbiome analysis tools

title: Getting Started with comparpq
text: package includes Glom_otu dataset, contains glomalean (arbuscular mycorrhizal fungi) OTU data:
code:
Glom_otu #> phyloseq-class experiment-level object #> otu_table()   OTU Table:         [ 1147 taxa and 444 samples ] #> sample_data() Sample Data:       [ 444 samples by 118 sample variables ] #> tax_table()   Taxonomy Table:    [ 1147 taxa by 14 taxonomic ranks ] #> refseq()      DNAStringSet:      [ 1147 reference sequences ] # Basic information about the dataset cat("Number of taxa:", phyloseq::ntaxa(Glom_otu), "
") #> Number of taxa: 1147 cat("Number of samples:", phyloseq::nsamples(Glom_otu), "
") #> Number of samples: 444 cat("Taxonomic ranks:", paste(phyloseq::rank_names(Glom_otu), collapse = ", "), "
") #> Taxonomic ranks: Kingdom, Phyla, Class, Order, Family, Genus, Species, Kingdom__eukaryome_Glomero, Phylum__eukaryome_Glomero, Class__eukaryome_Glomero, Order__eukaryome_Glomero, Family__eukaryome_Glomero, Genus__eukaryome_Glomero, Species__eukaryome_Glomero

title: Getting Started with comparpq
code:
# Select specific taxonomic ranks Glom_otu_subset <- select_ranks_pq(Glom_otu, Kingdom, Genus) cat("Original ranks:", paste(phyloseq::rank_names(Glom_otu), collapse = ", "), "
") #> Original ranks: Kingdom, Phyla, Class, Order, Family, Genus, Species, Kingdom__eukaryome_Glomero, Phylum__eukaryome_Glomero, Class__eukaryome_Glomero, Order__eukaryome_Glomero, Family__eukaryome_Glomero, Genus__eukaryome_Glomero, Species__eukaryome_Glomero cat("Selected ranks:", paste(phyloseq::rank_names(Glom_otu_subset), collapse = ", "), "
") #> Selected ranks: Kingdom, Genus # Rename taxonomic ranks renamed_physeq <- rename_ranks_pq(Glom_otu,   new_names = c(     "Domain", "Division", "Class_level",     "Order_level", "Family_level", "Genus_level"   ) ) cat("Renamed ranks:", paste(phyloseq::rank_names(renamed_physeq), collapse = ", "), "
") #> Renamed ranks: Kingdom, Phyla, Class, Order, Family, Genus, Species, Kingdom__eukaryome_Glomero, Phylum__eukaryome_Glomero, Class__eukaryome_Glomero, Order__eukaryome_Glomero, Family__eukaryome_Glomero, Genus__eukaryome_Glomero, Species__eukaryome_Glomero

title: Getting Started with comparpq
code: # Replace unwanted patterns with NA # This is useful for cleaning taxonomic assignments cleaned_physeq <- taxtab_replace_pattern_by_NA(Glom_otu,   pattern = "^uncultured",   taxonomic_ranks = "Genus" )  # Check how many taxa were affected original_genus <- phyloseq::tax_table(Glom_otu)[, "Genus"] cleaned_genus <- phyloseq::tax_table(cleaned_physeq)[, "Genus"] cat(   "Uncultured genera replaced with NA:",   sum(is.na(cleaned_genus)) - sum(is.na(original_genus)), "\n" ) #> Uncultured genera replaced with NA: 0

what: Creating Fake Taxa for Accuracy Assessment
title: Getting Started with comparpq
text: evaluate taxonomic assignment accuracy, can add fake taxa dataset:
code:
# Add shuffled sequences (creates fake taxa from existing sequences) physeq_with_shuffled <- add_shuffle_seq_pq(Glom_otu, n_fake = 10) cat("Original taxa count:", phyloseq::ntaxa(Glom_otu), "
") #> Original taxa count: 1147 cat("With fake taxa:", phyloseq::ntaxa(physeq_with_shuffled), "
") #> With fake taxa: 1157  # Add external sequences (simulates contamination or database gaps) external_seq <- Biostrings::readDNAStringSet(   system.file("extdata/ex_little.fasta", package = "MiscMetabar") ) physeq_with_external <- add_external_seq_pq(Glom_otu, ext_seqs = external_seq) cat("With external sequences added:", phyloseq::ntaxa(physeq_with_external), "
") #> With external sequences added: 1149

title: Getting Started with comparpq
code: # Example of computing accuracy metrics # This requires properly formatted comparison data  # Step 1: Prepare your comparison data structure # ranks_df should contain your taxonomic assignments to evaluate # true_values_df should contain the known correct assignments  # Example structure (replace with your actual data): # ranks_df <- data.frame( #   method1 = c("Fungi", "Ascomycota", "Sordariomycetes"), #   method2 = c("Fungi", "Basidiomycota", "Agaricomycetes") # ) # # true_values_df <- data.frame( #   Kingdom = c("Fungi", "Fungi"), #   Phylum = c("Glomeromycota", "Glomeromycota") # )  # Step 2: Compute metrics # accuracy_results <- tc_metrics_mock(physeq_with_shuffled, #                                   ranks_df = ranks_df, #                                   true_values_df = true_values_df, #                                   fake_taxa = TRUE)

title: Getting Started with comparpq
text: Prepare data: Start phyloseq object containing OTU/ASV data Add fake taxa: Use add_shuffle_seq_pq() add_external_seq_pq() create test cases Perform taxonomic assignments: Apply different methods/databases data Compare results: Use tc_metrics_mock() quantify accuracy Visualize: Create plots using bubbles_pq(), tc_bar(), tc_circle()

title: Getting Started with comparpq
code: # Example quality control workflow qc_physeq <- Glom_otu  # 1. Clean up problematic annotations qc_physeq <- taxtab_replace_pattern_by_NA(qc_physeq,   pattern = "unknown|unidentified|uncultured",   taxonomic_ranks = c("Family", "Genus") )  # 2. Select relevant taxonomic ranks qc_physeq <- select_ranks_pq(qc_physeq,   taxonomic_ranks = c("Kingdom", "Phyla", "Class", "Order", "Family") )  # 3. Standardize rank names qc_physeq <- rename_ranks_pq(qc_physeq,   old_names = c("Kingdom", "Phyla", "Class", "Order", "Family"),   new_names = c("Kingdom__", "Phyla__", "Class__", "Order__", "Family__") )  cat("Quality control completed. Final dataset has", phyloseq::ntaxa(qc_physeq), "taxa\n") #> Quality control completed. Final dataset has 1147 taxa

title: Getting Started with comparpq
text: Function documentation: Use ?function_name detailed help function Package website: Visit https://adrientaudiere.github.io/comparpq/ Report issues: https://github.com/adrientaudiere/comparpq/issues {MiscMetabar}: Extended microbiome analysis {phyloseq}: Core microbiome data handling

title: Getting Started with comparpq
text: comparpq package provides comprehensive toolkit : Interactive visualization phyloseq objects using modern web technologies Accuracy assessment taxonomic assignment methods using mock communities Data preprocessing quality control microbiome analyses Comparative analysis different taxonomic databases assignment approaches makes particularly valuable researchers working taxonomic assignment method development, database comparison, microbiome data quality assessment.

title: The list_phyloseq Class
text: list_phyloseq class S7 class designed store compare multiple phyloseq objects. automatically computes summary statistics phyloseq object determines type comparison based : Detected characteristics: sample overlap, nested samples, shared modalities User-provided parameters: same_primer_seq_tech same_bioinfo_pipeline vignette demonstrates: create list_phyloseq objects appropriate parameters six types comparisons use Utility functions manipulating list_phyloseq objects Visualization functions exploring presenting results

title: The list_phyloseq Class
code: devtools::load_all() # library(comparpq) library(phyloseq)

title: The list_phyloseq Class
text: simplest way create list_phyloseq pass named list phyloseq objects. can also specify same_primer_seq_tech same_bioinfo_pipeline parameters control comparison type: output shows: Summary table: Statistics phyloseq object (n_samples, n_taxa, n_sequences, etc.) Comparison characteristics: Type comparison, same_primer_seq_tech, same_bioinfo_pipeline, sample overlap, shared modalities
code: lpq <- list_phyloseq(list(   fungi = data_fungi,   fungi_mini = data_fungi_mini ))  lpq #> list_phyloseq object with 2 phyloseq objects #>  #> --- Summary --- #> # A tibble: 2 × 17 #>   name   n_samples n_taxa n_sequences n_occurence mean_seq_length min_seq_length #>   <chr>      <int>  <int>       <dbl>       <int>           <dbl>          <int> #> 1 fungi        185   1420     1839124       12499            318.            251 #> 2 fungi…       137     45      569525         560            343.            303 #> # ℹ 10 more variables: mean_seq_per_sample <dbl>, sd_seq_per_sample <dbl>, #> #   min_seq_per_sample <dbl>, max_seq_per_sample <dbl>, #> #   mean_seq_per_taxon <dbl>, sd_seq_per_taxon <dbl>, has_sam_data <lgl>, #> #   has_tax_table <lgl>, has_refseq <lgl>, has_phy_tree <lgl> #>  #> --- Comparison characteristics --- #> Type of comparison: NESTED_ROBUSTNESS  #> Nested samples detected (one phyloseq derived from another). #>   Nesting: fungi_mini (137 samples) is nested in fungi (185 samples) #>   Useful to test robustness to data processing (e.g., rarefaction). #>   Comparisons should focus on the common (nested) samples.  #>  #> Same primer/seq tech: TRUE  #> Same bioinfo pipeline: TRUE  #> Same sample_data structure: TRUE  #> Same samples: FALSE  #> Nested samples: TRUE  #> Same taxa: FALSE  #> Common samples: 137  #> Common taxa: 45

title: The list_phyloseq Class
text: creating list_phyloseq, can specify: same_primer_seq_tech (logical, default TRUE): Set FALSE comparing different primers (e.g., ITS1 vs ITS2) technologies (e.g., Illumina vs PacBio) same_bioinfo_pipeline (logical, default TRUE): Set FALSE comparing different clustering methods, taxonomic databases, analysis parameters

title: The list_phyloseq Class
text: list_phyloseq class determines type comparison based detected sample overlap user-provided same_primer_seq_tech same_bioinfo_pipeline parameters. six main types:

title: The list_phyloseq Class
text: Type | Samples | primer/seq_tech | pipeline | pipeline modality | Use Case | modality | Use Case | |——|————–|———————-|———————–|———-||———-| | REPRODUCIBILITY | Yes | TRUE | TRUE | TRUE | Test reproducibility identical analysis | | ROBUSTNESS | Yes | TRUE | FALSE | TRUE | Test effect different pipelines/parameters | | REPLICABILITY | Yes | FALSE | - | TRUE | Test effect different primers/technologies | | NESTED_ROBUSTNESS | Nested | - | - | TRUE | Test effect rarefaction/filtering | | EXPLORATION | | - | - | TRUE | Explore differences sample groups | | SEPARATE_ANALYSIS | | - | - | FALSE | Independent analyses (comparison may meaningful) |

title: The list_phyloseq Class
text: phyloseq objects samples, primer/technology (same_primer_seq_tech = TRUE), pipeline (same_bioinfo_pipeline = TRUE, defaults), comparison classified REPRODUCIBILITY. can also useful test effect stochasticity function.
code:
# REPRODUCIBILITY: Same samples, same pipeline (default parameters) # Example: Running the same analysis twice to test reproducibility lpq_repro <- list_phyloseq(list(   run1 = data_fungi,   run2 = data_fungi ))  cat("Type:", lpq_repro@comparison$type_of_comparison, "
") #> Type: REPRODUCIBILITY cat("Same primer/seq tech:", lpq_repro@comparison$same_primer_seq_tech, "
") #> Same primer/seq tech: TRUE cat("Same bioinfo pipeline:", lpq_repro@comparison$same_bioinfo_pipeline, "
") #> Same bioinfo pipeline: TRUE # REPRODUCIBILITY: Same samples, same pipeline (default parameters) # Example: Running the same analysis twice to test reproducibility lpq_repro <- list_phyloseq(list(   run1 = rarefy_even_depth(data_fungi, rngseed = 66),   run2 = rarefy_even_depth(data_fungi, rngseed = 42) ))  cat("Type:", lpq_repro@comparison$type_of_comparison, "
") #> Type: REPRODUCIBILITY cat("Same primer/seq tech:", lpq_repro@comparison$same_primer_seq_tech, "
") #> Same primer/seq tech: TRUE cat("Same bioinfo pipeline:", lpq_repro@comparison$same_bioinfo_pipeline, "
") #> Same bioinfo pipeline: TRUE

title: The list_phyloseq Class
text: phyloseq objects samples primer/technology, different pipelines (same_bioinfo_pipeline = FALSE), comparison classified ROBUSTNESS.
code:
# ROBUSTNESS: Same samples, different pipeline # Example: Comparing different clustering methods or taxonomic databases lpq_robust <- list_phyloseq(   list(     raw_fungi = data_fungi,     fungi_swarm = postcluster_pq(data_fungi, method = "swarm"),     fungi_mumu = mumu_pq(data_fungi)$new_physeq   ),   same_bioinfo_pipeline = FALSE # Different bioinformatics pipelines )  cat("Type:", lpq_robust@comparison$type_of_comparison, "
") #> Type: ROBUSTNESS cat("Same primer/seq tech:", lpq_robust@comparison$same_primer_seq_tech, "
") #> Same primer/seq tech: TRUE cat("Same bioinfo pipeline:", lpq_robust@comparison$same_bioinfo_pipeline, "
") #> Same bioinfo pipeline: FALSE

title: The list_phyloseq Class
text: phyloseq objects samples different primers technologies (same_primer_seq_tech = FALSE), comparison classified REPLICABILITY.
code: # REPLICABILITY: Same samples, different primer/technology # Example: Comparing ITS1 vs ITS2, or Illumina vs PacBio lpq_replic <- list_phyloseq(   list(     ITS1 = data_fungi,     ITS2 = data_fungi # In real use, this would be data from a different primer   ),   same_primer_seq_tech = FALSE # Different primers/technologies )  cat("Type:", lpq_replic@comparison$type_of_comparison, "\n") #> Type: REPLICABILITY cat("Same primer/seq tech:", lpq_replic@comparison$same_primer_seq_tech, "\n") #> Same primer/seq tech: FALSE

title: The list_phyloseq Class
text: one phyloseq object derived another (e.g., rarefied version), samples one subset samples another. type detected automatically regardless same_primer_seq_tech same_bioinfo_pipeline parameters. useful testing robustness data processing choices like rarefaction filtering. comparison focus common (nested) samples make meaningful comparisons. Use filter_common_lpq() (see Utility Functions).
code:
# Create a rarefied version (samples may be lost if they don't meet depth threshold) set.seed(123) data_fungi_rarefied <- rarefy_even_depth(data_fungi, sample.size = 1000, verbose = FALSE)  lpq_nested <- list_phyloseq(list(   original = data_fungi,   rarefied = data_fungi_rarefied ))  # Check the comparison type cat("Type:", lpq_nested@comparison$type_of_comparison, "
") #> Type: NESTED_ROBUSTNESS cat("Nested samples:", lpq_nested@comparison$nested_samples, "
") #> Nested samples: TRUE cat("Nesting structure:", lpq_nested@comparison$nesting_structure, "
") #> Nesting structure: rarefied (143 samples) is nested in original (185 samples)

title: The list_phyloseq Class
text: phyloseq objects different samples shared modalities sample_data (e.g., experimental conditions, sites), comparison classified EXPLORATION. type detected automatically based sample_data.
code:
# Simulate by subsetting samples from different parts of the dataset # In real use, these would be different sample groups with shared experimental factors sample_names_fungi <- sample_names(data_fungi) n_samples <- length(sample_names_fungi)  # Split samples into two groups (simulating different sample sets with shared modalities) group1_samples <- sample_names_fungi[1:floor(n_samples / 2)] group2_samples <- sample_names_fungi[(floor(n_samples / 2) + 1):n_samples]  data_group1 <- prune_samples(group1_samples, data_fungi) data_group2 <- prune_samples(group2_samples, data_fungi)  lpq_exploration <- list_phyloseq(list(   group1 = data_group1,   group2 = data_group2 ))  cat("Type:", lpq_exploration@comparison$type_of_comparison, "
") #> Type: EXPLORATION cat("Same samples:", lpq_exploration@comparison$same_samples, "
") #> Same samples: FALSE cat("Common samples:", lpq_exploration@comparison$n_common_samples, "
") #> Common samples: 0

title: The list_phyloseq Class
text: phyloseq objects different samples shared modalities, comparison classified SEPARATE_ANALYSIS. Direct comparison may meaningful. Separate analysis phyloseq object recommended.
code: # Example using completely different datasets data("enterotype", package = "phyloseq")  lpq_separate <- list_phyloseq(list(   fungi = data_fungi,   enterotype = enterotype ))  cat("Type:", lpq_separate@comparison$type_of_comparison, "\n") #> Type: SEPARATE_ANALYSIS cat("Same samples:", lpq_separate@comparison$same_samples, "\n") #> Same samples: FALSE

title: The list_phyloseq Class
code:
# Start with a single phyloseq lpq_single <- list_phyloseq(list(fungi = data_fungi)) cat("Initial number of phyloseq objects:", length(lpq_single), "
") #> Initial number of phyloseq objects: 1  # Add another phyloseq object lpq_added <- add_phyloseq(lpq_single, data_fungi_mini, name = "fungi_mini") cat("After adding:", length(lpq_added), "
") #> After adding: 2 cat("Names:", paste(names(lpq_added), collapse = ", "), "
") #> Names: fungi, fungi_mini  # Remove a phyloseq object lpq_removed <- remove_phyloseq(lpq_added, "fungi_mini") cat("After removing:", length(lpq_removed), "
") #> After removing: 1

title: The list_phyloseq Class
text: modify phyloseq objects inside list_phyloseq, use update_list_phyloseq() recompute summary table comparison characteristics. can also use function change same_primer_seq_tech same_bioinfo_pipeline parameters:
code: lpq@phyloseq_list[[1]] <- rarefy_even_depth(lpq@phyloseq_list[[1]]) lpq_updated <- update_list_phyloseq(lpq)  # Change the comparison type by modifying parameters lpq_robustness <- update_list_phyloseq(lpq, same_bioinfo_pipeline = FALSE) cat("New type:", lpq_robustness@comparison$type_of_comparison, "\n")

what: Filtering to Common Samples and Taxa
title: The list_phyloseq Class
text: filter_common_lpq() function filters phyloseq object retain samples /taxa common across objects. particularly useful : NESTED_ROBUSTNESS comparisons: filter common samples comparing original vs rarefied comparison need phyloseq objects samples/taxa can also filter common taxa:
code:
# Create a list_phyloseq with nested samples set.seed(123) lpq_to_filter <- list_phyloseq(list(   original = data_fungi,   rarefied = rarefy_even_depth(data_fungi, sample.size = 1000, verbose = FALSE) ))  # Before filtering cat("Before filtering:
") #> Before filtering: cat("  Original samples:", nsamples(lpq_to_filter[["original"]]), "
") #>   Original samples: 185 cat("  Rarefied samples:", nsamples(lpq_to_filter[["rarefied"]]), "
") #>   Rarefied samples: 143 cat("  Common samples:", lpq_to_filter@comparison$n_common_samples, "
") #>   Common samples: 143  # Filter to keep only common samples lpq_filtered <- filter_common_lpq(lpq_to_filter,   filter_samples = TRUE,   filter_taxa = FALSE,   verbose = TRUE )  # After filtering - both have the same samples cat("
After filtering:
") #>  #> After filtering: cat("  Original samples:", nsamples(lpq_filtered[["original"]]), "
") #>   Original samples: 143 cat("  Rarefied samples:", nsamples(lpq_filtered[["rarefied"]]), "
") #>   Rarefied samples: 143 cat("  Same samples now:", lpq_filtered@comparison$same_samples, "
") #>   Same samples now: TRUE # Filter both samples and taxa lpq_filtered_both <- filter_common_lpq(lpq_to_filter,   filter_samples = TRUE,   filter_taxa = TRUE )

title: The list_phyloseq Class
text: shared_mod_lpq() function displays sample_data variable modalities shared across phyloseq objects: useful understanding factors common across datasets, especially EXPLORATION type comparisons.
code: shared_mod_lpq(lpq) #> # A tibble: 5 × 3 #>   Variable     N_shared Shared_modalities                                        #>   <chr>           <int> <chr>                                                    #> 1 X                 137 A10-005-B_S188_MERGED.fastq.gz, A10-005-H_S189_MERGED.f… #> 2 Sample_names      137 A10-005-B_S188, A10-005-H_S189, A10-005-M_S190, A12-007… #> 3 Tree_name         100 A10-005, A12-007, A15-004, A8-005, AB29-abm-X, AC27-013… #> 4 Height              4 Low, High, Middle, NA                                    #> 5 Diameter           70 52, 28,4, 30,7, 32,8, 33,3, 99, 32, 55,4, 115,5, -, 10,…

title: The list_phyloseq Class
text: formattable_lpq() function creates beautiful HTML table showing summary statistics colored bars indicators: complete view including comparison characteristics:
code: # Basic formattable visualization formattable_lpq(lpq)  # Custom columns selection formattable_lpq(lpq, columns = c("name", "n_samples", "n_taxa", "n_sequences"))  # Custom colors for bars formattable_lpq(lpq, bar_colors = list(   n_samples = "steelblue",   n_taxa = "darkgreen" )) # Get both summary and comparison tables result <- formattable_lpq_full(lpq) result$summary result$comparison

title: The list_phyloseq Class
text: upset_lpq() function creates UpSet plots Venn diagrams showing shared taxonomic values across phyloseq objects:
code: # Create a list_phyloseq with multiple objects lpq_multi <- list_phyloseq(list(   fungi = data_fungi,   fungi_mini = data_fungi_mini ))  # Venn diagram (automatically chosen for ≤4 sets) upset_lpq(lpq_multi, tax_rank = "Family") # Force UpSet plot (better for more than 4 sets or complex intersections) data("enterotype", package = "phyloseq") lpq_many <- list_phyloseq(list(   fungi = data_fungi,   fungi_mini = data_fungi_mini,   fungi_rarefied = rarefy_even_depth(data_fungi, sample.size = 1000, verbose = FALSE),   enterotype = enterotype ))  upset_lpq(lpq_many, tax_rank = "Genus", plot_type = "upset")

title: The list_phyloseq Class
text: list_phyloseq class supports standard R subsetting operations:
code: # Get the number of phyloseq objects length(lpq) #> [1] 2  # Get names names(lpq) #> [1] "fungi"      "fungi_mini"  # Extract a single phyloseq object pq1 <- lpq[["fungi"]] cat("Extracted phyloseq has", ntaxa(pq1), "taxa\n") #> Extracted phyloseq has 1420 taxa  # Subset to create a new list_phyloseq with fewer objects lpq_subset <- lpq[1] # Returns a new list_phyloseq with only the first object

title: The list_phyloseq Class
text: ’s complete workflow comparing effect rarefaction data:
code: # Step 1: Create original and rarefied versions set.seed(42) original <- data_fungi rarefied <- rarefy_even_depth(data_fungi, sample.size = 1000, verbose = FALSE)  # Step 2: Create list_phyloseq lpq_rarefaction <- list_phyloseq(list(   original = original,   rarefied = rarefied ))  # Step 3: Check the comparison type print(lpq_rarefaction) #> list_phyloseq object with 2 phyloseq objects #>  #> --- Summary --- #> # A tibble: 2 × 17 #>   name   n_samples n_taxa n_sequences n_occurence mean_seq_length min_seq_length #>   <chr>      <int>  <int>       <dbl>       <int>           <dbl>          <int> #> 1 origi…       185   1420     1839124       12499            318.            251 #> 2 raref…       143   1397      143000        5900            318.            251 #> # ℹ 10 more variables: mean_seq_per_sample <dbl>, sd_seq_per_sample <dbl>, #> #   min_seq_per_sample <dbl>, max_seq_per_sample <dbl>, #> #   mean_seq_per_taxon <dbl>, sd_seq_per_taxon <dbl>, has_sam_data <lgl>, #> #   has_tax_table <lgl>, has_refseq <lgl>, has_phy_tree <lgl> #>  #> --- Comparison characteristics --- #> Type of comparison: NESTED_ROBUSTNESS  #> Nested samples detected (one phyloseq derived from another). #>   Nesting: rarefied (143 samples) is nested in original (185 samples) #>   Useful to test robustness to data processing (e.g., rarefaction). #>   Comparisons should focus on the common (nested) samples.  #>  #> Same primer/seq tech: TRUE  #> Same bioinfo pipeline: TRUE  #> Same sample_data structure: TRUE  #> Same samples: FALSE  #> Nested samples: TRUE  #> Same taxa: FALSE  #> Common samples: 143  #> Common taxa: 1397  # Step 4: Filter to common samples for fair comparison lpq_common <- filter_common_lpq(lpq_rarefaction,   filter_samples = TRUE,   verbose = TRUE )  # Step 5: Visualize shared taxa at different ranks upset_lpq(lpq_common, tax_rank = "Family") # Step 6: View the formatted summary formattable_lpq(lpq_common)

title: The list_phyloseq Class
text: list_phyloseq class provides powerful framework comparing multiple phyloseq objects: Precise comparison type determined same_primer_seq_tech same_bioinfo_pipeline parameters combined automatic detection sample overlap Summary statistics computed phyloseq object Utility functions adding, removing, filtering, updating Visualization functions exploring differences (formattable tables, UpSet/Venn plots) Key points: Set same_bioinfo_pipeline = FALSE ROBUSTNESS comparisons (different pipelines) Set same_primer_seq_tech = FALSE REPLICABILITY comparisons (different primers/technologies) NESTED_ROBUSTNESS, EXPLORATION, SEPARATE_ANALYSIS detected automatically NESTED_ROBUSTNESS comparisons, use filter_common_lpq() focus common samples upset_lpq() function excellent visualizing shared taxonomic composition Use update_list_phyloseq() change parameters recompute modifications

text: Always follow Conventional Commits specification (https://www.conventionalcommits.org/en/v1.0.0/) Format: [optional scope]: Types: feat, fix, docs, style, refactor, test, chore Example: “feat(api): add user authentication endpoint” Use lowercase type description Keep description concise imperative mood

title: NA
code: # To run code Rscript -e "devtools::load_all(); code"  # To run all tests Rscript -e "devtools::test()"  # To run all tests for files starting with {name} Rscript -e "devtools::test(filter = '^{name}')"  # To run all tests for R/{name}.R Rscript -e "devtools::test_active_file('R/{name}.R')"  # To run a single test "blah" for R/{name}.R Rscript -e "devtools::test_active_file('R/{name}.R', desc = 'blah')"  # To document the package Rscript -e "devtools::document()"  # To check pkgdown documentation Rscript -e "pkgdown::check_pkgdown()"  # To format code air format .

title: NA
text: Always run air format . generating code Use base pipe operator (|>) magrittr pipe (%>%) Don’t use _$x _$[["x"]] since dbplyr must work R 4.1. Use \() ... single-line anonymous functions. cases, use function() {...}

title: NA
text: Tests R/{name}.R go tests/testthat/test-{name}.R. new code accompanying test. existing tests, place new tests next similar existing tests. Strive keep tests minimal comments.

title: NA
text: Every user-facing function exported roxygen2 documentation. Wrap roxygen comments 80 characters. Internal functions roxygen documentation. Whenever add new (non-internal) documentation topic, also add topic _pkgdown.yml. Use pkgdown::check_pkgdown() check topics included reference index. @param x (Int, required) integer vector length 1 `@param y (Logical, default NULL) boolean factor.

title: NA
text: Every user-facing change given bullet NEWS.md. add bullets small documentation changes internal refactorings. bullet briefly describes change end user, mention related issue parentheses. bullet can consist multiple sentences contain new lines (.e. don’t wrap bullet). change related function, put name function early bullet. Order bullets alphabetically function name. Put bullets don’t mention function names beginning.

title: NA
text: Use sentence case headings. Use US English.

title: NA
text: user asks proofread file, act expert proofreader editor deep understanding clear, engaging, well-structured writing. Work paragraph paragraph, always starting making TODO list includes individual items top-level heading. Fix spelling, grammar, minor problems without asking user. Label unclear, confusing, ambiguous sentences FIXME comment. report changed.

what: An extension to {MiscMetabar} and {phyloseq} package to compare phyloseq object
title: An extension to {MiscMetabar} and {phyloseq} package to compare phyloseq object
text: comparpq extension {MiscMetabar} {phyloseq} packages provides tools comparing phyloseq objects. package focuses taxonomic comparison, accuracy metrics computation, interactive visualizations microbiome data analysis.

title: An extension to {MiscMetabar} and {phyloseq} package to compare phyloseq object
text: can install development version comparpq GitHub :
code: # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("adrientaudiere/comparpq")

title: An extension to {MiscMetabar} and {phyloseq} package to compare phyloseq object
text: tc_metrics_mock(): Compute accuracy metrics comparing taxonomic assignations mock communities tc_metrics_mock_vec(): Vectorized version efficient metric computation tc_points_matrix(): Create matrices taxonomic comparison visualization

title: An extension to {MiscMetabar} and {phyloseq} package to compare phyloseq object
text: bubbles_pq(): Interactive bubble plots using Observable HQ phyloseq objects tc_bar() & tc_circle(): Bar charts circular plots taxonomic comparisons rainplot_taxo_na(): Rain plots visualizing NA patterns taxonomic data

title: An extension to {MiscMetabar} and {phyloseq} package to compare phyloseq object
text: Fake taxa creation: add_external_seq_pq(), add_shuffle_seq_pq() mock community preparation Taxonomic table utilities: rename_ranks_pq(), select_ranks_pq(), resolve_taxo_conflict() Pattern replacement: taxtab_replace_pattern_by_NA() data cleaning

title: An extension to {MiscMetabar} and {phyloseq} package to compare phyloseq object
code: library(comparpq)  # Load example data data("Glom_otu")  # Create an interactive bubble plot bubbles_pq(Glom_otu,            rank_color = "Family",            min_nb_seq = 1000)  # For taxonomic comparison analysis with mock communities: # 1. First prepare your data with fake taxa physeq_with_fake <- add_shuffle_seq_pq(Glom_otu, nb_seq_fake = 50)  # 2. Perform taxonomic assignment (using your preferred method) # 3. Compare results against known taxonomy # tc_metrics_mock(physeq_with_fake, ranks_df, true_values_df)

what: PERMANOVA analysis on each phyloseq object in a list_phyloseq — adonis_lpq
title: PERMANOVA analysis on each phyloseq object in a list_phyloseq — adonis_lpq
text: Performs PERMANOVA analysis using MiscMetabar::adonis_pq() phyloseq object list_phyloseq returns summary table results.

title: PERMANOVA analysis on each phyloseq object in a list_phyloseq — adonis_lpq
code: adonis_lpq(   x,   formula,   dist_method = "bray",   na_remove = FALSE,   correction_for_sample_size = FALSE,   rarefy_nb_seqs = FALSE,   by = "margin",   verbose = TRUE,   ... )

title: PERMANOVA analysis on each phyloseq object in a list_phyloseq — adonis_lpq
text: x (required) list_phyloseq object. formula (character, required) right part formula vegan::adonis2(). Variables must present sample_data slot phyloseq objects. formula contain variables shared modalities. dist_method (character, default "bray") distance method use. See phyloseq::distance() available methods. na_remove (logical, default FALSE) TRUE, samples NA values formula variables removed analysis. correction_for_sample_size (logical, default FALSE) TRUE, adds library size formula following Weiss et al. 2017 recommendations. rarefy_nb_seqs (logical, default FALSE) TRUE, rarefy sample computing distances. (character, default "margin") argument passed vegan::adonis2(). Options "terms", "margin", NULL. verbose (logical, default TRUE) TRUE, print progress messages. ... Additional arguments passed MiscMetabar::adonis_pq().

title: PERMANOVA analysis on each phyloseq object in a list_phyloseq — adonis_lpq
text: tibble one row per phyloseq object following columns: name Name phyloseq object term term(s) formula (one row per term multiple) Df Degrees freedom SumOfSqs Sum squares R2 R-squared value F F statistic Pr(>F) P-value partial_R2 Partial R-squared (available)

title: PERMANOVA analysis on each phyloseq object in a list_phyloseq — adonis_lpq
text: function requires list_phyloseq type SEPARATE_ANALYSIS, formula must contain variables common across phyloseq objects. function wrapper around MiscMetabar::adonis_pq(), wraps vegan::adonis2().

title: PERMANOVA analysis on each phyloseq object in a list_phyloseq — adonis_lpq

what: Bubble plot of phyloseq object with observablehq   — bubbles_pq
title: Bubble plot of phyloseq object with observablehq   — bubbles_pq
text: Creates interactive bubble plot visualization taxa abundances phyloseq object using Observable notebook.

title: Bubble plot of phyloseq object with observablehq   — bubbles_pq
code: bubbles_pq(   physeq,   rank_label = "Taxa",   rank_color = "Family",   categorical_scheme = "d3.schemeCategory10",   label_color = "grey10",   value_color = "grey20",   label_size = 8,   log1ptransform = FALSE,   min_nb_seq = 0,   randomize = FALSE,   seed = 32,   width = 600,   include = c("TitleCell", "key", "chart"),   notebook = "https://observablehq.com/d/d755af3197af2320",   return_dataframe = FALSE,   title = "",   title_size = "22px" )

title: Bubble plot of phyloseq object with observablehq   — bubbles_pq
text: physeq (required) phyloseq-class object obtained using phyloseq package. rank_label (character, default "Taxa") name column @tax_table slot label points. set "Taxa", taxa name used. rank_color (character, default "Family") name column @tax_table slot color points. categorical_scheme (character, default "d3.schemeCategory10") color scheme d3js. label_color (character, default "grey10") Color label text. value_color (character, default "grey20") Color value text. label_size (integer, default 8) Font size label. log1ptransform (logical, default FALSE) TRUE, number sequences log1p transformed. min_nb_seq (integer, default 0) Minimum number sequences filter taxa low abundance. randomize (logical, default FALSE) TRUE, shuffles order taxa. seed (integer, default 32) Seed randomization. width (integer, default 600) notebook width visualization pixels. include (character vector, default c("TitleCell", "key", "chart")) can modify include remove title /legend, example using include = c("chart"). notebook (character, default "https://observablehq.com/d/d755af3197af2320") can change notebook url know . return_dataframe (logical, default FALSE) TRUE, plot returned, resulting dataframe plot returned. title (character, default "") Title plot. title_size (character, default "22px") Font size title.

code: filter_common_lpq(   x,   filter_samples = TRUE,   filter_taxa = FALSE,   clean = TRUE,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Filter phyloseq objects to keep only shared samples and/or taxa — filter_common_lpq
text: x (required) list_phyloseq object. filter_samples (logical, default TRUE) TRUE, filter keep samples present phyloseq objects. filter_taxa (logical, default FALSE) TRUE, filter keep taxa present phyloseq objects. clean (logical, default TRUE) TRUE, apply MiscMetabar::clean_pq() filtering remove empty samples/taxa. verbose (logical, default TRUE) TRUE, print information filtering process.

title: Filter phyloseq objects to keep only shared samples and/or taxa — filter_common_lpq
text: new list_phyloseq object filtered phyloseq objects

title: Filter phyloseq objects to keep only shared samples and/or taxa — filter_common_lpq
text: function particularly useful : NESTED_ROBUSTNESS comparisons: filter common samples comparing original vs rarefied data EXPLORATION comparisons: filter common samples/taxa comparing different sample groups comparison need ensure phyloseq objects contain samples taxa

title: Filter phyloseq objects to keep only shared samples and/or taxa — filter_common_lpq
code: lpq <- list_phyloseq(list(   original = data_fungi,   mini = data_fungi_mini,   rarefied = rarefy_even_depth(data_fungi) )) #> You set `rngseed` to FALSE. Make sure you've set & recorded #>  the random seed of your session for reproducibility. #> See `?set.seed` #> ... #> 1014OTUs were removed because they are no longer  #> present in any sample after random subsampling #> ...  # Filter to keep only common samples (useful for nested comparisons) lpq_filtered <- filter_common_lpq(lpq, filter_samples = TRUE, verbose = FALSE) #> Cleaning suppress 10 taxa and 0 samples. #> Cleaning suppress 60 taxa and 0 samples.  # Filter to keep only common taxa lpq_filtered <- filter_common_lpq(lpq, filter_samples = FALSE, filter_taxa = TRUE) #> Filtering to 42 common taxa. #> Cleaning suppress 0 taxa and 49 samples. #>   original: 185 -> 136 samples, 1420 -> 42 taxa #> Cleaning suppress 0 taxa and 1 samples. #>   mini: 137 -> 136 samples, 45 -> 42 taxa #> Cleaning suppress 0 taxa and 107 samples. #>   rarefied: 185 -> 78 samples, 406 -> 42 taxa  # Filter both samples and taxa lpq_filtered <- filter_common_lpq(lpq, filter_samples = TRUE, filter_taxa = TRUE) #> Filtering to 137 common samples. #> Filtering to 42 common taxa. #> Cleaning suppress 0 taxa and 1 samples. #>   original: 185 -> 136 samples, 1420 -> 42 taxa #> Cleaning suppress 0 taxa and 1 samples. #>   mini: 137 -> 136 samples, 45 -> 42 taxa #> Cleaning suppress 0 taxa and 59 samples. #>   rarefied: 185 -> 78 samples, 406 -> 42 taxa

what: Automated model selection for Hill diversity on each phyloseq in a list_phyloseq — glmulti_lpq
title: Automated model selection for Hill diversity on each phyloseq in a list_phyloseq — glmulti_lpq
text: Performs automated model selection multimodel inference using MiscMetabar::glmutli_pq() phyloseq object list_phyloseq. Returns summary table results phyloseq objects.

title: Automated model selection for Hill diversity on each phyloseq in a list_phyloseq — glmulti_lpq
code: glmulti_lpq(   x,   formula,   fitfunction = "lm",   hill_scales = c(0, 1, 2),   aic_step = 2,   confsetsize = 100,   plotty = FALSE,   level = 1,   method = "h",   crit = "aicc",   verbose = TRUE,   ... )

title: Automated model selection for Hill diversity on each phyloseq in a list_phyloseq — glmulti_lpq
text: x (required) list_phyloseq object. formula (character, required) model formula glmulti. Variables must present sample_data slot phyloseq objects. Hill numbers (Hill_0, Hill_1, Hill_2) Abundance automatically available. fitfunction (character, default "lm") model fitting function use. Options include "lm" linear models "glm" generalized linear models. hill_scales (numeric vector, default c(0, 1, 2)) q values Hill number computation. Defaults Hill numbers 0 (richness), 1 (Shannon exponential), 2 (inverse Simpson). aic_step (numeric, default 2) AIC score threshold model selection. Models within threshold best model included. confsetsize (integer, default 100) number models return confidence set. plotty (logical, default FALSE) TRUE, display IC profile glmulti search. level (integer, default 1) Model complexity level. 1 main effects , 2 pairwise interactions. method (character, default "h") search method glmulti. Options: "h" (exhaustive), "g" (genetic algorithm), "l" (branch--bound), "d" (summary ). crit (character, default "aicc") Information criterion model selection. Options include "aic", "aicc" (small-sample corrected AIC), "bic". verbose (logical, default TRUE) TRUE, print progress messages. ... Additional arguments passed MiscMetabar::glmutli_pq().

title: Automated model selection for Hill diversity on each phyloseq in a list_phyloseq — glmulti_lpq
text: tibble combined results phyloseq objects, containing following columns: name Name phyloseq object variable variable name model estimates model coefficient estimate unconditional_interval Confidence interval model averaging nb_model Number models containing variable importance Relative importance variable (sum Akaike weights) alpha Significance level

title: Automated model selection for Hill diversity on each phyloseq in a list_phyloseq — glmulti_lpq
text: function requires list_phyloseq type SEPARATE_ANALYSIS, formula must contain variables common across phyloseq objects. function wraps MiscMetabar::glmutli_pq(), wraps glmulti package automated model selection. phyloseq object, Hill diversity indices computed used response variables model selection process.

title: Automated model selection for Hill diversity on each phyloseq in a list_phyloseq — glmulti_lpq

what: S7 class for comparing phyloseq objects — list_phyloseq
title: S7 class for comparing phyloseq objects — list_phyloseq
text: class store compare multiple phyloseq objects. contains: list phyloseq objects summary table computed characteristics phyloseq list comparison characteristics phyloseq objects S7 class store compare multiple phyloseq objects.

title: S7 class for comparing phyloseq objects — list_phyloseq
code: list_phyloseq(   physeq_list,   names = NULL,   same_primer_seq_tech = TRUE,   same_bioinfo_pipeline = TRUE )

title: S7 class for comparing phyloseq objects — list_phyloseq
text: physeq_list (required) named list phyloseq objects. names (character vector, default NULL) Optional names phyloseq objects. NULL list unnamed, names generated automatically. same_primer_seq_tech (logical, default TRUE) Whether primer sequencing technology used across phyloseq objects. Set FALSE comparing different primers (e.g., ITS1 vs ITS2) technologies (e.g., Illumina vs PacBio). same_bioinfo_pipeline (logical, default TRUE) Whether bioinformatics pipeline used across phyloseq objects. Set FALSE comparing different clustering methods, taxonomic databases, analysis parameters.

title: S7 class for comparing phyloseq objects — list_phyloseq
text: combination same_primer_seq_tech same_bioinfo_pipeline along detected sample overlap determines comparison type:

title: S7 class for comparing phyloseq objects — list_phyloseq
text: phyloseq_list named list phyloseq objects summary_table tibble summarizing phyloseq object comparison list characteristics comparing phyloseq objects

title: S7 class for comparing phyloseq objects — list_phyloseq
text: list_phyloseq class determines type comparison based : Detected characteristics (sample overlap, nested samples, shared modalities) User-provided parameters (same_primer_seq_tech same_bioinfo_pipeline) six main types comparisons: REPRODUCIBILITY pipeline (same_bioinfo_pipeline = TRUE), primer/technology (same_primer_seq_tech = TRUE), samples. Used test reproducibility results running exact analysis multiple times. ROBUSTNESS Different pipeline (same_bioinfo_pipeline = FALSE, e.g., different clustering method, different assignment database) primer/technology (same_primer_seq_tech = TRUE), samples. Used test robustness conclusions methodological choices. NESTED_ROBUSTNESS One phyloseq object derived another, samples subset (e.g., rarefied version created rarefy_even_depth()). Used test robustness data processing choices like rarefaction, filtering, subsetting. Comparisons focus common (nested) samples. REPLICABILITY Different primer /technology (same_primer_seq_tech = FALSE, e.g., ITS1 vs ITS2, Illumina vs PacBio), samples. Used test replicability across taxonomic groups sequencing technologies. EXPLORATION Different samples shared modalities. Useful explore differences among groups samples. Note: consider merging samples one phyloseq object analyses instead. SEPARATE_ANALYSIS Different samples shared modalities. Separate analysis phyloseq object recommended direct comparison may meaningful.

title: S7 class for comparing phyloseq objects — list_phyloseq
code: # REPRODUCIBILITY: Same samples, same pipeline (default) lpq_repro <- list_phyloseq(list(run1 = data_fungi, run2 = data_fungi))  # ROBUSTNESS: Same samples, different pipeline lpq_robust <- list_phyloseq(   list(method_A = data_fungi, method_B = data_fungi),   same_bioinfo_pipeline = FALSE )  # REPLICABILITY: Same samples, different primer/technology lpq_replic <- list_phyloseq(   list(ITS1 = data_fungi, ITS2 = data_fungi),   same_primer_seq_tech = FALSE )

text: Useful compare taxonomy two different source/db/algo

title: Rainplot of the nb taxa assigned (not NA) — rainplot_taxo_na

title: Rainplot of the nb taxa assigned (not NA) — rainplot_taxo_na
text: physeq (required) phyloseq-class object obtained using phyloseq package. ranks (character integer vector, default NULL) ranks include rainplot. left NULL, ranks used. rank can defined either integer index full names (exactly matching colnames tax_table slot). min_nb_seq (integer, default 0) Minimum number sequences filter taxa low abundance. merge_sample_by (character, default NULL) vector determine samples merge using MiscMetabar::merge_samples2() function. Need physeq@sam_data. sample_colored (logical, default FALSE) TRUE, points colored samples. sample_linked (logical, default FALSE) TRUE, points linked samples. ... Additional arguments passed ggrain::geom_rain().

title: Rainplot of the nb taxa assigned (not NA) — rainplot_taxo_na
code: rainplot_taxo_na(subset_taxa_pq(Glom_otu, taxa_sums(Glom_otu) > 5000),   ranks = c("Family", "Family__eukaryome_Glomero") ) #> Cleaning suppress 0 taxa (  ) and 1 sample(s) ( samp_Blanc-PCR-racines ). #> Number of non-matching ASV 0 #> Number of matching ASV 1147 #> Number of filtered-out ASV 955 #> Number of kept ASV 192 #> Number of kept samples 443  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ rainplot_taxo_na(Glom_otu)  Glom_otu@sam_data$tmt_type <- paste0(Glom_otu@sam_data$Tmt, "_", Glom_otu@sam_data$Type) rainplot_taxo_na(   Glom_otu,   merge_sample_by = "tmt_type",   sample_colored = TRUE,   sample_linked = TRUE ) rainplot_taxo_na(Glom_otu, ranks = c(4, 12), rain.side = "f1x1") rainplot_taxo_na(   Glom_otu,   ranks = c(6, 14),   rain.side = "f1x1",   sample_linked = TRUE ) +   theme(legend.position = "none") } # }

what: Resolve taxonomic conflict in the tax_table of a phyloseq object — resolve_taxo_conflict
title: Resolve taxonomic conflict in the tax_table of a phyloseq object — resolve_taxo_conflict
text: Resolve taxonomic conflict tax_table phyloseq object

title: Resolve taxonomic conflict in the tax_table of a phyloseq object — resolve_taxo_conflict
code: resolve_taxo_conflict(   physeq,   pattern_tax_ranks = NULL,   method = c("consensus", "rel_majority", "abs_majority", "preference", "unanimity"),   strict = FALSE,   second_method = c("consensus", "rel_majority", "abs_majority", "preference",     "unanimity"),   nb_agree_threshold = 1,   keep_tax_ranks = TRUE,   new_names = NULL,   preference_pattern = NULL,   collapse_string = "/",   replace_collapsed_rank_by_NA = FALSE )

title: Resolve taxonomic conflict in the tax_table of a phyloseq object — resolve_taxo_conflict
text: physeq (required) phyloseq object. pattern_tax_ranks (character vector, default NULL) vector patterns aggregate taxonomic ranks. example "^Genus" stands taxonomic ranks (columns tax_table slot) starting Genus. method (character, default "consensus") One "consensus", "rel_majority", "abs_majority", "preference" "unanimity". See details documentation function MiscMetabar::resolve_vector_ranks(). strict (logical, default FALSE) TRUE, NA considered informative resolving conflict (.e. NA taken account vote). See details information. second_method (character, default "consensus") One "consensus", "rel_majority", "abs_majority", "unanimity". used method = "preference". See details. nb_agree_threshold (integer, default 1) minimum number times value must arise selected using vote. 2, taxonomic values present least 2 times vector kept. keep_tax_ranks (logical, default TRUE) TRUE, keep old taxonomic ranks result. new_names (character vector, default NULL) vector new names taxonomic columns. preference_pattern (character, default NULL) pattern match column used preferred one method = "preference". collapse_string (character, default "/") character collapse taxonomic names multiple assignment done. replace_collapsed_rank_by_NA (logical, default FALSE) TRUE, multiple assignments (taxonomic rank including 'collapse_string' parameter) replaced NA.

what: Convert taxonomy dataframe to phylogenetic tree — taxo2tree
title: Convert taxonomy dataframe to phylogenetic tree — taxo2tree
text: Creates phylo object taxonomy table hierarchical taxonomic ranks columns.

title: Convert taxonomy dataframe to phylogenetic tree — taxo2tree
code: taxo2tree(   physeq,   ranks = c("Domain", "Phylum", "Class", "Order", "Family", "Genus", "Species"),   internal_node_singletons = TRUE,   use_taxa_names = TRUE )

title: Convert taxonomy dataframe to phylogenetic tree — taxo2tree
text: physeq (required) phyloseq-class object ranks Character vector specifying column names use taxonomic ranks, ordered highest lowest. default: c("Domain", "Phylum", "Class", "Order", "Family", "Genus", "Species"). internal_node_singletons Logical, TRUE, create internal nodes singleton. FALSE, internal nodes one descendant discarded. use_taxa_names Logical, TRUE (default), use taxa names (rownames, e.g., ASV_1, ASV_2) terminal leaves. FALSE, collapse identical taxonomy paths use lowest rank value tip labels. useful creating cleaner trees show taxonomic structure without individual ASV/OTU names.

title: Convert taxonomy dataframe to phylogenetic tree — taxo2tree
text: phylo object (ape package) representing taxonomic tree.

title: Convert taxonomy dataframe to phylogenetic tree — taxo2tree
text: Adrien Taudière

title: Convert taxonomy dataframe to phylogenetic tree — taxo2tree
code: data_fungi_mini@phy_tree <-   phyloseq::phy_tree(taxo2tree(data_fungi_mini,     ranks = c(       "Domain", "Phylum", "Class", "Order",       "Family", "Genus", "Genus_species"     )   )) #> Found more than one class "phylo" in cache; using the first, from namespace 'phyloseq' #> Also defined by ‘tidytree’  library(ggtree)  ggtree(data_fungi_mini@phy_tree) +   geom_nodelab(size = 2, nudge_x = -0.2, nudge_y = 0.6) +   geom_tiplab() #> Found more than one class "phylo" in cache; using the first, from namespace 'phyloseq' #> Also defined by ‘tidytree’   psm <- psmelt(data_fungi_mini) |>   group_by(OTU) |>   summarize(     Mol_Abund = sum(Abundance),     Frequency = sum(Abundance > 0),     Class = tidyr::replace_na(unique(Class), "Unknown")   )   ggtree(data_fungi_mini@phy_tree) %<+% psm +   geom_tiplab(offset = .6, hjust = .5) +   geom_tippoint(aes(     shape = Class,     color = log10(1 + Mol_Abund),     size = Frequency   )) +   geom_nodelab(nudge_x = -0.2, nudge_y = 0.6) +   scale_color_viridis_c() #> Found more than one class "phylo" in cache; using the first, from namespace 'phyloseq' #> Also defined by ‘tidytree’    # Without internal node singletons and only until the Genus level tree_wo_singletons <-   phyloseq::phy_tree(taxo2tree(data_fungi_mini,     ranks = c(       "Domain", "Phylum", "Class", "Order",       "Family", "Genus"     ),     internal_node_singletons = FALSE   )) #> Found more than one class "phylo" in cache; using the first, from namespace 'phyloseq' #> Also defined by ‘tidytree’  ggtree::ggtree(tree_wo_singletons) +   ggtree::geom_nodelab(size = 2, nudge_x = -0.2, nudge_y = 0.6) +   ggtree::geom_tiplab() #> Found more than one class "phylo" in cache; using the first, from namespace 'phyloseq' #> Also defined by ‘tidytree’   # Without taxa names (collapse identical paths) tree_no_taxa <- taxo2tree(data_fungi_mini,   ranks = c("Domain", "Phylum", "Class", "Order", "Family", "Genus"),   use_taxa_names = FALSE ) #> Found more than one class "phylo" in cache; using the first, from namespace 'phyloseq' #> Also defined by ‘tidytree’  ggtree::ggtree(tree_no_taxa) +   ggtree::geom_nodelab(size = 2, nudge_x = -0.2, nudge_y = 0.6) +   ggtree::geom_tiplab() #> Found more than one class "phylo" in cache; using the first, from namespace 'phyloseq' #> Also defined by ‘tidytree’

title: Barchart of ratio to compare 2 taxonomic ranks — tc_bar
text: physeq (required) phyloseq-class object obtained using phyloseq package. rank_1 (character integer) Define first taxonomic rank number name column tax_table slot. rank_2 (character integer) Define second taxonomic rank number name column tax_table slot. color_rank (character integer) Define taxonomic rank color number name column tax_table slot. point_size (numeric, default 0.3) Size points. point_alpha (numeric, default 0.3) Transparency points. merge_sample_by (character, default NULL) vector determine samples merge using MiscMetabar::merge_samples2() function. Need physeq@sam_data. log10trans (logical, default TRUE) TRUE, abundance log10 transformed.

title: Barchart of ratio to compare 2 taxonomic ranks — tc_bar
text: ggplot2 object

title: Barchart of ratio to compare 2 taxonomic ranks — tc_bar
text: Adrien Taudière

title: Barchart of ratio to compare 2 taxonomic ranks — tc_bar
code: tc_bar(subset_taxa_pq(Glom_otu, taxa_sums(Glom_otu) > 5000), rank_1 = 5, rank_2 = 13, color_rank = 3) #> Cleaning suppress 0 taxa (  ) and 1 sample(s) ( samp_Blanc-PCR-racines ). #> Number of non-matching ASV 0 #> Number of matching ASV 1147 #> Number of filtered-out ASV 955 #> Number of kept ASV 192 #> Number of kept samples 443  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ tc_bar(Glom_otu, rank_1 = 5, rank_2 = 13, color_rank = 3) tc_bar(as_binary_otu_table(Glom_otu), rank_1 = 5, rank_2 = 13, color_rank = 3, log10trans = FALSE) tc_bar(Glom_otu,   rank_1 = "Genus",   rank_2 = "Genus__eukaryome_Glomero",   color_rank = "Family" ) } # }

what: Compute congruence metrics between two taxonomic assignments — tc_congruence_metrics
title: Compute congruence metrics between two taxonomic assignments — tc_congruence_metrics
text: Computes metrics quantifying congruence two taxonomic assignments set taxa (ASVs/OTUs). useful comparing different taxonomic databases, assignment methods, reference versions.

title: Compute congruence metrics between two taxonomic assignments — tc_congruence_metrics
code: tc_congruence_metrics(physeq_1, physeq_2 = NULL, ranks_1, ranks_2)

title: Compute congruence metrics between two taxonomic assignments — tc_congruence_metrics
text: physeq_1 (required) phyloseq-class object first taxonomic assignment. physeq_2 (phyloseq, default NULL) phyloseq-class object second taxonomic assignment. NULL, uses physeq_1 (useful comparing different rank columns object). ranks_1 (character vector, required) Taxonomic rank names use first assignment. Must match column names physeq_1's tax_table. ranks_2 (character vector, required) Taxonomic rank names use second assignment. Must match column names physeq_2's tax_table.

title: Compute congruence metrics between two taxonomic assignments — tc_congruence_metrics
text: list following components: summary data frame counts percentages category, including leaf_match_total counts taxa deepest classification matches regardless path only_in_1 Character vector taxa names present physeq_1 only_in_2 Character vector taxa names present physeq_2 classified_only_1 Character vector common taxa classified physeq_1 (NA physeq_2 specified ranks) classified_only_2 Character vector common taxa classified physeq_2 (NA physeq_1 specified ranks) unclassified_both Character vector common taxa NA physeq objects congruent Character vector taxa identical taxonomic paths (values ranks, NA positions) partial_1_deeper Character vector taxa physeq_1 classifies deeper rank agrees shared ranks partial_2_deeper Character vector taxa physeq_2 classifies deeper rank agrees shared ranks incongruent_leaves Character vector taxa disagreement deepest (leaf) level assignments incongruent_nodes Character vector taxa disagreement higher (internal node) levels, even leaves match details data frame per-taxon details including: taxon name, depth_1, depth_2, leaf_1, leaf_2, leaf_match (TRUE leaves equal), category

what: Plot two taxonomy trees with linked correspondences — tc_linked_trees
title: Plot two taxonomy trees with linked correspondences — tc_linked_trees
text: Creates visualization two taxonomy trees facing , matching taxa aligned horizontally. Optionally draws lines connecting matching leaf taxa. Useful comparing taxonomic assignments different databases classification methods.

title: Plot two taxonomy trees with linked correspondences — tc_linked_trees
code: tc_linked_trees(   physeq_1,   physeq_2 = NULL,   ranks_1,   ranks_2,   tree_distance = 1,   show_tip_links = TRUE,   link_by_taxa = FALSE,   link_alpha = 0.5,   link_color = "grey50",   show_labels = TRUE,   label_size = 2,   internal_node_singletons = FALSE,   use_taxa_names = FALSE )

title: Plot two taxonomy trees with linked correspondences — tc_linked_trees
text: physeq_1 (required) phyloseq-class object left tree. physeq_2 (phyloseq, default NULL) phyloseq-class object right tree. NULL, uses physeq_1 (useful comparing different rank columns object). ranks_1 (character vector, required) Taxonomic rank names use first tree. Must match column names physeq_1's tax_table. ranks_2 (character vector, required) Taxonomic rank names use second tree. Must match column names physeq_2's tax_table. tree_distance (numeric, default 1) Distance two trees. show_tip_links (logical, default TRUE) Whether draw lines matching leaf taxa (tips). Internal nodes never linked. link_by_taxa (logical, default FALSE) TRUE, links drawn based taxa (ASV/OTU) correspondence: taxon physeq_1, link drawn corresponding tip tree_2 based taxonomy. Line width proportional number taxa sharing link. Requires physeq_1 physeq_2 taxa names (rownames). link_alpha (numeric, default 0.5) Transparency correspondence lines. link_color (character, default "grey50") Color correspondence lines. Use NULL color label. show_labels (logical, default TRUE) Whether show node/tip labels. label_size (numeric, default 2) Base size labels. Labels scaled depth: shallower nodes (closer root) get larger text (1.5x), deeper nodes get smaller text (0.6x). labels positioned branch avoid overlap. internal_node_singletons (logical, default FALSE) Whether create internal nodes singletons. Passed taxo2tree. use_taxa_names (logical, default FALSE) Whether use taxa names (e.g., ASV_1, ASV_2) terminal leaves. FALSE (default), collapses identical taxonomy paths uses lowest rank value tip labels. Passed taxo2tree.

title: Plot two taxonomy trees with linked correspondences — tc_linked_trees
text: ggplot2 object can customized.

title: Plot two taxonomy trees with linked correspondences — tc_linked_trees
text: Adrien Taudière

title: Plot two taxonomy trees with linked correspondences — tc_linked_trees
code: # Compare two taxonomic assignments from the same phyloseq object # using different rank columns (physeq_2 defaults to physeq_1) tc_linked_trees(   Glom_otu,   ranks_1 = c("Kingdom", "Class", "Order", "Family"),   ranks_2 = c(     "Kingdom__eukaryome_Glomero", "Class__eukaryome_Glomero",     "Order__eukaryome_Glomero", "Family__eukaryome_Glomero"   ) ) #> Found more than one class "phylo" in cache; using the first, from namespace 'phyloseq' #> Also defined by ‘tidytree’ #> Found more than one class "phylo" in cache; using the first, from namespace 'phyloseq' #> Also defined by ‘tidytree’   if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Link by taxa with line width proportional to ASV count tc_linked_trees(   Glom_otu,   ranks_1 = c("Kingdom", "Class", "Order", "Family"),   ranks_2 = c(     "Kingdom__eukaryome_Glomero", "Class__eukaryome_Glomero",     "Order__eukaryome_Glomero", "Family__eukaryome_Glomero"   ),   link_by_taxa = TRUE )  # Without tip links tc_linked_trees(   Glom_otu,   ranks_1 = c("Kingdom", "Class", "Order", "Family"),   ranks_2 = c(     "Kingdom__eukaryome_Glomero", "Class__eukaryome_Glomero",     "Order__eukaryome_Glomero", "Family__eukaryome_Glomero"   ),   show_tip_links = FALSE )  # Color links by label tc_linked_trees(   Glom_otu,   ranks_1 = c("Kingdom", "Class", "Order", "Family"),   ranks_2 = c(     "Kingdom__eukaryome_Glomero", "Class__eukaryome_Glomero",     "Order__eukaryome_Glomero", "Family__eukaryome_Glomero"   ),   link_color = NULL ) +   theme(legend.position = "none")  # Using one phyloseq object for both trees # and different rank levels with link_by_taxa tc_linked_trees(   Glom_otu,   ranks_1 = c("Kingdom", "Class", "Order", "Family"),   ranks_2 = c(     "Kingdom__eukaryome_Glomero", "Class__eukaryome_Glomero",     "Order__eukaryome_Glomero", "Family__eukaryome_Glomero"   ), link_color = NULL,   link_by_taxa = TRUE, ) +   theme(legend.position = "none") } # }

title: Compute accuracy metrics of taxonomic assignation using a mock (known) community for one rank — tc_metrics_mock_vec
text: physeq (required) phyloseq-class object obtained using phyloseq package. taxonomic_rank (required) Name (number) taxonomic rank count. true_values (required) vector true taxonomic assignation fake_taxa (logical, default TRUE) TRUE, fake_pattern vector used identify fake taxa, .e. taxa reference database (see add_external_seq_pq()) taxa fake sequences (see add_shuffle_seq_pq()). fake_pattern (character vector, default c("^fake_", "^external_")) vector patterns used identify fake taxa using regex search name. verbose (logical, default TRUE) TRUE, print informative messages.

title: Compute accuracy metrics of taxonomic assignation using a mock (known) community for one rank — tc_metrics_mock_vec
text: list metrics (see confusion matrix article wikipedia): TP (number true positive) FP (number false positive) FN (number false negative) FDR (false discovery rate) = FP / (FP + TP) TPR (true positive rate, also named recall sensitivity) PPV (positive predictive value, also named precision) = TP / (TP + FP) F1_score (F1 score) = 2 * TP / (2 * TP + FP + FN) fake taxa present fake_taxa true, metrics computed: TN (number true negative) ACC (Accuracy) = (TP + TN) / (TP + TN + FP + FN) MCC (Matthews correlation coefficient) = (TP * TN - FP * FN) / sqrt((TP + FP) * (TP + FN) * (FP + TN) * (TN + FN))

title: Compute accuracy metrics of taxonomic assignation using a mock (known) community for one rank — tc_metrics_mock_vec
text: Adrien Taudière
